data.narrative:
Pyka is a Series B autonomous aircraft company in the Drones & Autonomous sector, having raised $40M to date. The valuation universe consists of 7 private peers in the same sector, including Elroy Air, Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Manna Aero; no public comparables exist in this narrow space.

Given the nascent state of the sector and limited same-stage peer density, a peer-median approach was not applicable. The analysis instead relied on stage-progression methodology, applying a 1.9x multiplier to the $40M total raised proxy to reflect typical capital step-ups between Series B and subsequent rounds. This method yielded an implied valuation of $76M. The private comp universe proved too dispersed across stage and valuation tiers to support reliable median triangulation, and public benchmarks do not exist for this category.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $65M to $87M (median $76M), centered on the stage-progression result. Key uncertainties include the lack of sufficient narrow-stage peer data, the heterogeneity of business models and maturity across the Drones & Autonomous sector, and absence of public market signals. Any valuation within this range should be stress-tested against company-specific milestones, competitive positioning, and path to profitability given the capital intensity and regulatory complexity of autonomous aircraft development.
